assertions.description: Display schematic correctly identifies 27 total components (26 in-BOM + G2 which is in_bom=false), 3 power rails (3.3V, GND, VUSB), 38 nets, 8 capacitors, 5 resistors, 4 connectors, 4 fiducials, 1 IC, 1 LED, 1 switch, 1 jumper, 2 other.

The RC low-pass filter formed by R2 (10k) and C7 (1.0uF) connected to the RESET net of U1 (SSD1306 OLED) is correctly detected. Cutoff frequency of 15.92 Hz (1/(2π×10kΩ×1μF)) is correct. Input net is 3.3V, output net is RESET, ground net is GND.

The OLED U1 is correctly identified as an I2C device with connections on ESP_21/SDA and ESP_22/SCL. The has_pullup=false flag is correct since no pull-up resistors appear in this sub-board schematic (they reside on the main board).

The decoupling_analysis correctly identifies C4 (1.0uF), C6 (1.0uF), and C16 (0.1uF) as decoupling capacitors for the 3.3V rail, totaling 2.1uF. has_bulk=true, has_bypass=true, has_high_freq=false is accurate. C1 (2.2uF) is correctly omitted as it connects to U1's VCC pin separately.

INCORRECT: G2 (OSHW_Logo) in the Display schematic has in_bom=no and on_board=yes. The analyzer includes it in components and counts it in total_components=27 although it is a board-only graphic with no electrical function. The true BOM component count is 26. This is the same issue as the Connector schematic with G3.

The microSD connector J2 is connected via SPI signals SD_COPI, SD_CIPO, SD_SCK, and SD_~{CS} (global labels). These constitute a clear SPI interface. The bus_analysis.spi list is empty and no memory_interfaces are detected. The absence is because no SPI controller IC is present in this sub-schematic—the signals enter via global labels from the main board. While structurally understandable, the SPI bus topology is unobserved.
